titleStmt.title: Letter from James Davidson to his mother, Clara Mary Davidson, 31 December 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: James Davidson

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from James Davidson (1877-1916) to his mother, Clara Mary. In this letter to his mother, Davison reflects on the news of his family's Christmas. He writes that the 36th Division is being relocated back to their previous location. He is despondent about the continuous moves (c 10 times within three months of their arriving in France). He comments that the 36th Division is sent in 'to clean up a district', hence their being referred to as 'Carsons Travelling Menagerie'. He closes the letter in anticipation of receiving khaki mufflers and mittens so they can be distributed to some of the men. He wishes his grandmother and the rest of the family a happy new year.This letter is part of a large correspondence from Captain James Davidson to his parents. Davidson, who was Managing Director of the Sirocco Engineering Works in Belfast, served with 13 Royal Irish Rifles and the Machine Gun Corps. His letters describe his experiences while training in England and serving in France. He was killed on 1 July 1916, the first day of the Battle of the Somme.

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
31/12/15 My dearest Mother â It will soon be
1916 but I am very tired this evening &
don't intend to sit up & see the New
Year in. Your letter of 28th arrived
today â it was a quiet Xmas for you
all this year but I am glad Mr & Mrs
Vard were able to be with you. We
all thought we would be remaining
here for some little time to come but
orders have just come that we are to
move back to our previous area next
week â probably Thurs. or Friday. Isn't
it sickening! We had just got things
nicely fixed up here & now have to return
to uncomfortable village quarters. I
think this must be about our 10th move
since we arrived in France 3 months
ago. When any district requires to be
cleaned up the 36th Div. always seems
to be selected to do the dirty work. No
wonder we are known as Carsons travelling
menagerie. With love &
every good wish for the coming year to you
dear mother & all at home. Your loving son James
On Active Service
Rcd 4th Jany/16 Mrs. S.C.Davidson, Seacourt, Bangor, Co Down, Ireland
J Davidson
